repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Range Rover Sport P440e — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Assess battery health; module replacement may be possible
Replace all six — simple, effective preventative maintenance
Software update — almost always the fix
Worth it on a vehicle worth £70,000–£120,000
Replacement often £6,500–£10,000 — assess vehicle value carefully

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Range Rover Sport P440e? Check These First
buyIf: * Full Land Rover service history with documented battery health checks
* Recent software updates applied (PCM, BMS, TCU)
* Low-mileage with documented charging history (AC charging preferred)
* Still under the 3-year/80,000-mile battery warranty (check transferability)
avoidIf: * Significant reduction in electric-only range (battery degradation warning)
* Any hybrid system warnings or drive mode malfunctions without a documented fix
* Poor charging history (frequent rapid charging or deep discharges)
* Missing service history — the PHEV system is complex and unforgiving

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The P440e is a landmark powertrain — combining the refined 3.0-litre Ingenium petrol engine with a powerful electric motor for 440PS of seamless performance. The combustion engine is the same well-developed unit as the P400, while the hybrid system adds electric range and lower running costs. Battery health is the long-term question mark — buy on documented history, charge it properly, and this is a genuinely compelling plug-in hybrid.

cta.label: → Compare Range Rover Sport P440e powertrain prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Range Rover Sport P440e (or before you commit to a powertrain replacement), check these:
items: * Battery Health — Check the estimated electric range; any significant drop from the 51-mile (WLTP) claim?
* Charging History — Has the vehicle been charged correctly? Frequent rapid charging can degrade batteries
* Cold Start — Listen for any rattle on startup (timing chain stretch warning)
* Service History — Must show hybrid system checks; 12,000 mile oil changes are essential
* Misfire Check — Any hesitation, rough idle, or engine warning light? (coil failure)
* Hybrid System — Check all drive modes work; any warning lights or messages?
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Coil and hybrid system codes are common
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures

items.question: How much does a Land Rover Range Rover Sport P440e powertrain replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A reconditioned Range Rover Sport P440e powertrain (AJ20P6 + e-motor) typically costs £6,500–£10,000 supply only. Fitting adds £1,800–£2,500. Hybrid system work requires specialist training. Battery replacement is a separate cost — approximately £8,000–£12,000.

items.question: Is the Land Rover Range Rover Sport P440e reliable?
items.answer: The P440e combines the proven AJ20P6 3.0-litre Ingenium petrol engine with a 105kW electric motor. The combustion engine is well-developed and reliable, with ignition coils as the main wear item. The hybrid system adds complexity, but early owner reports indicate strong reliability. Battery health is the long-term question mark — all lithium-ion batteries degrade over time.

items.question: What are the common problems with the Range Rover Sport P440e?
items.answer: PHEV battery degradation (reduced electric range, £8,000–£12,000 replacement), ignition coil failure on the combustion engine (misfire, rough idle, £350–£600 repair), and hybrid system software issues (drive mode malfunctions, warning lights, £150–£500 software update).

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Range Rover Sport P440e powertrain, or should I scrap the car?
items.answer: Range Rover Sport P440e values range from £60,000–£120,000 depending on year, condition and mileage. A £6,500–£10,000 reconditioned powertrain represents 5–15% of vehicle value — well within our 60% Land Rover scrap threshold. For most examples, replacement is financially viable. Battery replacement at £8,000–£12,000 is a separate consideration.

items.question: How do I find my Land Rover Range Rover Sport P440e engine code?
items.answer: The P440e uses the AJ20P6 engine code — JLR's 3.0-litre Ingenium inline-six — combined with a 105kW electric motor. This is the same engine as the P400 but with additional hybrid hardware. The total system output is 440PS. The code is stamped on the engine block; your VIN and vehicle documentation will also confirm the specification.

items.question: Can I repair the battery degradation issue, or does it need replacement?
items.answer: In many cases, individual battery modules can be replaced (£2,000–£4,000 per module) rather than the entire pack (£8,000–£12,000). A Land Rover specialist can perform a battery health check to determine which cells have degraded. At the first sign of significant range reduction, book a health check. Regular AC charging (rather than rapid DC charging) and avoiding deep discharges help preserve battery life.
